seo.title: Context Window Management for AI Agents
seo.description: Complete guide to context window management for ai agents. Learn detection methods, fixes, and best practices for production AI agents.

content.intro: Context window management is one of the most critical and frequently mishandled challenges in building reliable AI agents. As agents process long conversations, retrieve documents, and chain tool calls, they rapidly consume their available token budget — leading to truncated reasoning, forgotten instructions, and silent failures. Without deliberate context management strategies, even well-designed agents degrade unpredictably as tasks grow in complexity.

whatIsIt.heading: What Is Context Window Management for AI Agents?
whatIsIt.explanation:
Every large language model has a fixed context window — a maximum number of tokens it can process in a single inference call. For AI agents, this window must simultaneously hold the system prompt, conversation history, tool definitions, retrieved documents, intermediate reasoning steps, and the current user request. Unlike a simple chatbot, agents accumulate context aggressively across multi-step tasks, meaning the window fills up far faster than most developers anticipate.

When an agent's context window fills, one of several failure modes occurs: the model provider returns a hard error and the agent crashes, the runtime silently truncates older messages causing the agent to lose critical instructions or prior decisions, or the model's attention degrades on distant tokens leading to subtle reasoning errors that are nearly impossible to debug. All three outcomes compromise reliability, and the silent truncation case is particularly dangerous because the agent appears to keep running while operating on incomplete information.

Effective context window management means proactively monitoring token usage, making deliberate decisions about what information to retain versus summarize or evict, and structuring prompts so that the most critical information survives any necessary compression. It is not a one-time configuration but an ongoing architectural concern that must be designed into the agent's core loop from the start.

examples.scenario: A customer support agent handles a long troubleshooting session. After 30 back-and-forth messages, the original system prompt instructions about tone, escalation policy, and product constraints are silently truncated from the context.
examples.consequence: The agent begins giving responses that violate company policy, offers unauthorized refunds, and loses the thread of the customer's original complaint — all without any error being raised.

examples.scenario: A research agent retrieves five large documents via RAG to answer a complex question, stuffing all raw text directly into the context alongside the full conversation history.
examples.consequence: The combined token count exceeds the model's limit, causing a hard API error that crashes the agent mid-task, losing all intermediate work and requiring a full restart.

examples.scenario: A coding agent is asked to refactor a large codebase across multiple tool calls. Each tool response appends full file contents to the context, and after ten iterations the window is 90% full.
examples.consequence: The model's attention on the original refactoring requirements degrades, causing it to introduce regressions and contradict earlier decisions it made in the same session.

examples.scenario: An autonomous planning agent runs a multi-hour task loop. No summarization or eviction strategy is in place, so every observation, action, and reflection is appended verbatim to the growing context.
examples.consequence: Token costs grow quadratically with task length, the agent eventually hits the context limit and halts, and the operator has no way to resume the task because no compressed state was persisted.

methods.name: Token Budget Instrumentation
methods.description: Instrument every LLM call to log the exact prompt token count, completion token count, and remaining budget as a percentage of the model's maximum context length. Set threshold alerts at 70%, 85%, and 95% utilization. This gives you real-time visibility into how quickly context fills across different task types and lets you catch runaway accumulation before it causes failures.

methods.name: Behavioral Regression Testing on Long Sessions
methods.description: Create a test suite that replays known multi-turn conversations of increasing length and checks whether the agent's responses remain consistent with its system prompt instructions. If the agent starts violating rules it followed correctly at turn 5 by turn 25, silent truncation is the likely culprit. Automate these tests to run on every deployment.

methods.name: Context Composition Auditing
methods.description: At each agent step, log a breakdown of what is consuming tokens: system prompt, conversation history, tool schemas, retrieved documents, and scratchpad. This reveals which component is the primary driver of context growth and where compression efforts should be focused. Many teams discover that tool schema definitions alone consume 15-20% of their budget.

methods.name: Error Rate Monitoring for Context-Length Exceptions
methods.description: Monitor your LLM API error logs specifically for context-length-exceeded errors (e.g., OpenAI error code context_length_exceeded, Anthropic's prompt-too-long). Track these as a dedicated reliability metric separate from general API errors. A rising rate of these errors is a direct signal that your context management strategy is insufficient for real-world usage patterns.

strategies.name: Sliding Window with Pinned System Prompt
strategies.description: Implement a message eviction strategy that always preserves the system prompt and the most recent N messages, evicting the oldest non-pinned messages when a token budget threshold is crossed. This prevents the most dangerous failure mode — losing system instructions — while keeping recent context intact.
strategies.code:
import tiktoken
from typing import List, Dict, Any

MAX_TOKENS = 128000
SAFETY_BUFFER = 8000  # Reserve for completion
TOKEN_BUDGET = MAX_TOKENS - SAFETY_BUFFER

def count_tokens(messages: List[Dict], model: str = "gpt-4o") -> int:
    enc = tiktoken.encoding_for_model(model)
    total = 0
    for msg in messages:
        # 4 tokens overhead per message
        total += 4 + len(enc.encode(msg.get("content", "")))
    return total

def sliding_window_context(
    system_prompt: str,
    history: List[Dict],
    new_message: Dict,
    model: str = "gpt-4o"
) -> List[Dict]:
    """
    Returns a context list that fits within TOKEN_BUDGET.
    Always pins the system prompt. Evicts oldest history messages first.
    """
    system_msg = {"role": "system", "content": system_prompt}
    candidate = [system_msg] + history + [new_message]

    while count_tokens(candidate, model) > TOKEN_BUDGET and len(candidate) > 2:
        # Remove the oldest non-system message (index 1)
        evicted = candidate.pop(1)
        print(f"[ContextManager] Evicted message: role={evicted['role']}, "
              f"preview='{evicted['content'][:60]}...'")

    if count_tokens(candidate, model) > TOKEN_BUDGET:
        raise ValueError(
            "Context budget exceeded even after full history eviction. "
            "System prompt or new message is too large."
        )

    return candidate

# Usage
context = sliding_window_context(
    system_prompt="You are a helpful assistant. Always follow company policy.",
    history=conversation_history,
    new_message={"role": "user", "content": user_input}
)
response = client.chat.completions.create(model="gpt-4o", messages=context)

strategies.name: Progressive Summarization of History
strategies.description: Instead of evicting messages entirely, use the LLM itself to compress older conversation segments into a dense summary that preserves key facts, decisions, and constraints. Inject this summary as a special context message. This retains semantic continuity across long sessions while dramatically reducing token consumption.
strategies.code:
from openai import OpenAI
from typing import List, Dict

client = OpenAI()

SUMMARIZATION_THRESHOLD = 20  # Summarize when history exceeds N messages
MESSAGES_TO_KEEP_VERBATIM = 6  # Always keep the last N messages as-is

def summarize_history_segment(
    messages: List[Dict],
    model: str = "gpt-4o-mini"  # Use cheaper model for summarization
) -> str:
    """Compress a list of messages into a concise summary."""
    formatted = "\n".join(
        f"{m['role'].upper()}: {m['content']}" for m in messages
    )
    summary_prompt = [
        {
            "role": "system",
            "content": (
                "You are a context summarizer. Given a conversation segment, "
                "produce a dense factual summary that preserves: key decisions made, "
                "important facts established, user preferences stated, errors encountered, "
                "and any constraints or requirements mentioned. Be concise but complete."
            )
        },
        {
            "role": "user",
            "content": f"Summarize this conversation segment:\n\n{formatted}"
        }
    ]
    response = client.chat.completions.create(
        model=model,
        messages=summary_prompt,
        max_tokens=500
    )
    return response.choices[0].message.content

def compress_history(
    history: List[Dict],
    existing_summary: str = ""
) -> tuple[List[Dict], str]:
    """
    If history is long, summarize the older portion and return
    (recent_messages, updated_summary).
    """
    if len(history) <= SUMMARIZATION_THRESHOLD:
        return history, existing_summary

    # Split: old segment to summarize, recent to keep verbatim
    old_segment = history[:-MESSAGES_TO_KEEP_VERBATIM]
    recent = history[-MESSAGES_TO_KEEP_VERBATIM:]

    new_summary_text = summarize_history_segment(old_segment)

    # Merge with any existing summary
    if existing_summary:
        combined_summary = (
            f"EARLIER CONTEXT SUMMARY:\n{existing_summary}\n\n"
            f"RECENT CONTEXT SUMMARY:\n{new_summary_text}"
        )
    else:
        combined_summary = new_summary_text

    print(f"[ContextManager] Compressed {len(old_segment)} messages into summary "
          f"({len(combined_summary)} chars)")
    return recent, combined_summary

def build_context_with_summary(
    system_prompt: str,
    summary: str,
    recent_history: List[Dict],
    new_message: Dict
) -> List[Dict]:
    messages = [{"role": "system", "content": system_prompt}]
    if summary:
        messages.append({
            "role": "system",
            "content": f"[CONVERSATION SUMMARY - treat as established context]\n{summary}"
        })
    messages.extend(recent_history)
    messages.append(new_message)
    return messages

strategies.name: Selective RAG Chunking and Re-ranking
strategies.description: Never inject full retrieved documents into the context. Instead, chunk documents into small segments, embed them, retrieve only the top-K most relevant chunks for the current query, and re-rank them by relevance score before injection. Set a hard token cap on the total retrieved content budget.
strategies.code:
from openai import OpenAI
from typing import List, Tuple
import numpy as np

client = OpenAI()

MAX_RETRIEVAL_TOKENS = 4000  # Hard cap on retrieved content
CHUNK_SIZE = 400  # tokens per chunk
TOP_K = 10  # Initial retrieval pool
FINAL_K = 4  # After re-ranking, keep this many

def chunk_document(text: str, chunk_size: int = CHUNK_SIZE) -> List[str]:
    """Split document into overlapping chunks by approximate word count."""
    words = text.split()
    # Approximate: 1 token ≈ 0.75 words
    word_chunk_size = int(chunk_size * 0.75)
    overlap = word_chunk_size // 5
    chunks = []
    start = 0
    while start < len(words):
        end = min(start + word_chunk_size, len(words))
        chunks.append(" ".join(words[start:end]))
        start += word_chunk_size - overlap
    return chunks

def embed_texts(texts: List[str]) -> List[List[float]]:
    response = client.embeddings.create(
        model="text-embedding-3-small",
        input=texts
    )
    return [item.embedding for item in response.data]

def cosine_similarity(a: List[float], b: List[float]) -> float:
    a_arr, b_arr = np.array(a), np.array(b)
    return float(np.dot(a_arr, b_arr) / (np.linalg.norm(a_arr) * np.linalg.norm(b_arr)))

def retrieve_relevant_chunks(
    query: str,
    documents: List[str],
    max_tokens: int = MAX_RETRIEVAL_TOKENS
) -> str:
    """
    Chunk documents, embed, retrieve top-K, re-rank, and return
    a token-capped string of the most relevant content.
    """
    # Chunk all documents
    all_chunks = []
    for doc in documents:
        all_chunks.extend(chunk_document(doc))

    if not all_chunks:
        return ""

    # Embed query and all chunks
    query_embedding = embed_texts([query])[0]
    chunk_embeddings = embed_texts(all_chunks)

    # Score and rank chunks
    scored: List[Tuple[float, str]] = [
        (cosine_similarity(query_embedding, emb), chunk)
        for emb, chunk in zip(chunk_embeddings, all_chunks)
    ]
    scored.sort(key=lambda x: x[0], reverse=True)

    # Select top chunks within token budget
    selected_chunks = []
    token_count = 0
    approx_tokens_per_word = 1.33

    for score, chunk in scored[:FINAL_K]:
        chunk_tokens = int(len(chunk.split()) * approx_tokens_per_word)
        if token_count + chunk_tokens > max_tokens:
            break
        selected_chunks.append(chunk)
        token_count += chunk_tokens
        print(f"[RAG] Selected chunk (score={score:.3f}, ~{chunk_tokens} tokens)")

    return "\n\n---\n\n".join(selected_chunks)

# Usage in agent loop
retrieved_context = retrieve_relevant_chunks(
    query=user_query,
    documents=fetched_documents
)
injected_content = f"[RETRIEVED CONTEXT]\n{retrieved_context}\n[END RETRIEVED CONTEXT]"

strategies.name: Stateful Context Manager with Persistent Checkpointing
strategies.description: Implement a dedicated ContextManager class that tracks token usage in real time, enforces budget policies, and persists compressed state to external storage. This enables long-running agents to resume tasks after context resets without losing progress, and gives operators full observability into context consumption.
strategies.code:
import json
import time
from dataclasses import dataclass, field, asdict
from typing import List, Dict, Optional
import tiktoken

@dataclass
class ContextState:
    session_id: str
    system_prompt: str
    summary: str = ""
    recent_history: List[Dict] = field(default_factory=list)
    total_messages_processed: int = 0
    peak_token_usage: int = 0
    created_at: float = field(default_factory=time.time)
    last_updated: float = field(default_factory=time.time)

class ContextManager:
    def __init__(
        self,
        session_id: str,
        system_prompt: str,
        model: str = "gpt-4o",
        max_context_tokens: int = 128000,
        safety_buffer: int = 8000,
        summarize_threshold: int = 15,
        verbatim_keep: int = 5,
        storage_path: Optional[str] = None
    ):
        self.model = model
        self.encoder = tiktoken.encoding_for_model(model)
        self.token_budget = max_context_tokens - safety_buffer
        self.summarize_threshold = summarize_threshold
        self.verbatim_keep = verbatim_keep
        self.storage_path = storage_path

        self.state = ContextState(
            session_id=session_id,
            system_prompt=system_prompt
        )

    def _count_tokens(self, text: str) -> int:
        return len(self.encoder.encode(text))

    def _count_messages_tokens(self, messages: List[Dict]) -> int:
        return sum(4 + self._count_tokens(m.get("content", "")) for m in messages)

    def get_token_usage(self) -> Dict[str, int]:
        messages = self._build_messages()
        used = self._count_messages_tokens(messages)
        return {
            "used": used,
            "budget": self.token_budget,
            "remaining": self.token_budget - used,
            "utilization_pct": int((used / self.token_budget) * 100)
        }

    def _build_messages(self, new_message: Optional[Dict] = None) -> List[Dict]:
        messages = [{"role": "system", "content": self.state.system_prompt}]
        if self.state.summary:
            messages.append({
                "role": "system",
                "content": f"[SESSION SUMMARY]\n{self.state.summary}"
            })
        messages.extend(self.state.recent_history)
        if new_message:
            messages.append(new_message)
        return messages

    def add_message(self, message: Dict) -> List[Dict]:
        """Add a message and return the current context ready for LLM call."""
        self.state.recent_history.append(message)
        self.state.total_messages_processed += 1
        self.state.last_updated = time.time()

        # Trigger summarization if history is long
        if len(self.state.recent_history) > self.summarize_threshold:
            self._compress_history()

        context = self._build_messages()
        token_usage = self._count_messages_tokens(context)
        self.state.peak_token_usage = max(self.state.peak_token_usage, token_usage)

        utilization = (token_usage / self.token_budget) * 100
        print(f"[ContextManager] session={self.state.session_id} "
              f"tokens={token_usage}/{self.token_budget} ({utilization:.1f}%) "
              f"messages={len(self.state.recent_history)}")

        if utilization > 90:
            print(f"[ContextManager] WARNING: Context at {utilization:.1f}% capacity")

        if self.storage_path:
            self._checkpoint()

        return context

    def _compress_history(self):
        """Summarize old history, keep recent messages verbatim."""
        from openai import OpenAI
        compress_client = OpenAI()

        old = self.state.recent_history[:-self.verbatim_keep]
        recent = self.state.recent_history[-self.verbatim_keep:]

        formatted = "\n".join(
            f"{m['role'].upper()}: {m['content']}" for m in old
        )
        response = compress_client.chat.completions.create(
            model="gpt-4o-mini",
            messages=[
                {"role": "system", "content": "Summarize this conversation segment, preserving all key facts, decisions, and requirements."},
                {"role": "user", "content": formatted}
            ],
            max_tokens=400
        )
        new_summary = response.choices[0].message.content

        if self.state.summary:
            self.state.summary = f"{self.state.summary}\n\n{new_summary}"
        else:
            self.state.summary = new_summary

        self.state.recent_history = recent
        print(f"[ContextManager] Compressed {len(old)} messages into summary")

    def _checkpoint(self):
        path = f"{self.storage_path}/{self.state.session_id}.json"
        with open(path, "w") as f:
            json.dump(asdict(self.state), f, indent=2)

    @classmethod
    def load_from_checkpoint(cls, session_id: str, storage_path: str, **kwargs) -> "ContextManager":
        path = f"{storage_path}/{session_id}.json"
        with open(path) as f:
            data = json.load(f)
        manager = cls(
            session_id=session_id,
            system_prompt=data["system_prompt"],
            storage_path=storage_path,
            **kwargs
        )
        manager.state = ContextState(**data)
        print(f"[ContextManager] Resumed session {session_id} "
              f"({data['total_messages_processed']} messages processed)")
        return manager

# Usage
ctx = ContextManager(
    session_id="user-123-task-456",
    system_prompt="You are a helpful coding assistant. Always write tests.",
    storage_path="./checkpoints"
)

# In agent loop:
context_messages = ctx.add_message({"role": "user", "content": user_input})
response = openai_client.chat.completions.create(
    model="gpt-4o",
    messages=context_messages
)
ctx.add_message({"role": "assistant", "content": response.choices[0].message.content})
print(ctx.get_token_usage())

tips.title: Always Reserve a Completion Buffer
tips.description: Never allow your prompt to consume 100% of the context window. Reserve at least 10-15% of the total token budget for the model's completion. A prompt that fills the entire window will either error out or produce a truncated response. Set your effective token budget to max_context_tokens minus your expected maximum completion length, and treat this as a hard ceiling in all context management logic.

tips.title: Treat Tool Schemas as First-Class Token Consumers
tips.description: Tool and function definitions injected into the context can consume thousands of tokens before any conversation content is added. Audit your tool schemas regularly, remove unused tools from the context when they are not relevant to the current task phase, and consider using compressed schema representations. In multi-step agents, dynamically inject only the tools needed for the current step rather than the full tool library.

tips.title: Use Cheaper Models for Summarization
tips.description: When compressing conversation history, use a smaller, faster, and cheaper model (e.g., GPT-4o-mini or Claude Haiku) rather than your primary reasoning model. Summarization is a straightforward task that does not require frontier model capabilities, and doing so reduces both latency and cost. The summarized output is then fed back into the primary model's context as a compressed representation.

tips.title: Log Context Composition at Every Step
tips.description: Maintain structured logs that record the token breakdown by component (system prompt, summary, history, tools, retrieved content, new message) at every agent step. This data is invaluable for debugging degraded behavior, optimizing token allocation, and capacity planning. Without this visibility, diagnosing context-related failures is largely guesswork.

tips.title: Design for Context Reset Resilience
tips.description: Assume that any long-running agent will eventually need to reset its context — whether due to hitting the window limit, a crash, or a deliberate restart. Design your agent's state management so that all critical information is persisted externally (task goals, completed steps, key findings) and can be reconstructed into a fresh context without loss of progress. Treat context as ephemeral working memory, not as the source of truth for agent state.

cta.heading: Detect and Fix This Automatically with Vex
cta.description: Vex automatically monitors token utilization across every agent session, alerts you before context limits cause failures, and provides built-in summarization and eviction policies so your agents stay reliable at any task length.
